title: Charting the Growth of Social-Physical HRI (spHRI): A Systematic Review Pipeline Augmented by Small Language Models
abstract: arXiv:2606.26382v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Social-physical human-robot interaction (spHRI) has grown rapidly across robotics, human-computer interaction, human-robot interaction, and haptics. Yet, fragmented terminology and inconsistent methodologies make systematic synthesis difficult. To support scalable review practices, we evaluated the extent to which small language models (SLMs; < 1.5B parameters) can assist with title and abstract screening for a large spHRI systematic review. While no SLMs matched human reviewers' performance, the models operated locally and screened papers orders of magnitude faster. The combined SLM ensemble identified 39 papers reviewers missed, representing 10.29% of the final relevant dataset. These results demonstrate that SLMs can augment, rather than replace, expert reviewers and make large-scale literature reviews accessible and sustainable.

Title: Charting the Growth of Social-Physical HRI (spHRI): A Systematic Review Pipeline Augmented by Small Language Models
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26382v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Social-physical human-robot interaction (spHRI) has grown rapidly across robotics, human-computer interaction, human-robot interaction, and haptics. Yet, fragmented terminology and inconsistent methodologies make systematic synthesis difficult. To support scalable review practices, we evaluated the extent to which small language models (SLMs; < 1.5B parameters) can assist with title and abstract screening for a large spHRI systematic review. While no SLMs matched human reviewers' performance, the models operated locally and screened papers orders of magnitude faster. The combined SLM ensemble identified 39 papers reviewers missed, representing 10.29% of the final relevant dataset. These results demonstrate that SLMs can augment, rather than replace, expert reviewers and make large-scale literature reviews accessible and sustainable.
translated_title: 社会-物理人机交互（spHRI）的发展轨迹：一种由小型语言模型增强的系统性综述流程
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26382v1 公告类型：交叉 摘要：社会-物理人机交互（spHRI）在机器人学、人机交互、人机交互和触觉学领域迅速发展。然而，术语碎片化和方法不一致使得系统性综合变得困难。为了支持可扩展的综述实践，我们评估了小型语言模型（SLMs；参数少于15亿）在大型spHRI系统综述中辅助标题和摘要筛选的程度。虽然没有任何SLM能达到人类评审员的性能，但这些模型在本地运行，且筛选论文的速度快了几个数量级。SLM集成模型识别出了评审员遗漏的39篇论文，占最终相关数据集的10.29%。这些结果表明，SLM可以增强而非取代专家评审员，并使大规模文献综述变得可获取和可持续。
tldr: 本文证明了小型语言模型（SLMs）能高效辅助社会-物理人机交互（spHRI）领域的系统综述，虽不及人类评审员性能，但能快速筛选并发现专家遗漏的论文，从而增强而非取代人工评审。
en_tdlr: This paper demonstrates that small language models (SLMs) can efficiently assist systematic reviews in social-physical human-robot interaction (spHRI), identifying papers missed by experts and augmenting rather than replacing human reviewers, despite not matching human performance.
